annotations.finding: IF epk-bukst = epk-bukrs â€” only propagates GSBER for same-company postings. Intercompany counterpart lines get NO GSBER.
annotations.impact: Any intercompany posting where partner company has no fallback BusA derivation will fail

annotations.finding: CLEAR epk-gsber on vendor lines with koart=K and ktosl=HRP/HRV
annotations.impact: Vendor clearing lines lose GSBER even if set upstream

annotations.finding: epk-gsber = accountingobjects-bus_area_empl â€” restores GSBER for cross-company TAX lines only (stazf=X), NOT vendor clearing lines
annotations.impact: Tax G/L lines get GSBER restored, vendor lines do not

annotations.finding: gsber_a parameter (I_GSBER_A) controls whether GSBER derivation runs at all. If not X, GSBER is cleared.
RW609 is NOT a Travel validation â€” it wraps BAPI_ACC_EMPLOYEE_PAY_POST errors. The actual rejection is inside the BAPI during FI document simulation.
annotations.impact: Misleading error message â€” looks like Travel module rejects, but its the FI BAPI

annotations.finding: PTRV_TRANSLATE is a FM in function group HRTS, not a standalone program. Sets gsber_a/gsber_g parameters that control GSBER derivation in LHRTSF01.
BAdI reads PA0027 subtype 02 to derive GSBER when gsbst is initial. CHECK at line 37 EXITS if no subtype 02 record found â€” does not raise error, silently skips.
annotations.impact: If PA0027-02 is expired or missing, GSBER stays empty with no warning

annotations.finding: CHECK sy-subrc IS INITIAL â€” exits method without error if PA0027-02 not found. No log, no warning.
annotations.impact: Invisible failure â€” BAdI appears to work but does nothing

annotations.finding: IIEP doc type: FR01 location gets TV, all others get TF. Changed by Konakov 2015-09-30.
WHEN OTHERS block â€” inst_fund_ba_wbs_cc is COMMENTED OUT for all non-UNES company codes (IIEP, IBE, ICBA, ICTP, MGIE, UIL, UIS). Commented by Konakov 2018.
annotations.impact: Non-UNES company codes have no BusA derivation from fund/WBS in this exit. They rely entirely on SAP standard (line 852) or GGB1.

annotations.finding: UNES company code: FUND_BA_WBS_CC is ACTIVE â€” derives BusA from fund, WBS, cost center.
GGB1 substitution rule for IIEP travel: only covers GL 2021042 and 2021061. GL 2021011 (SCSA vendor recon account) is NOT covered.
annotations.impact: Any IIEP vendor with AKONT=2021011 in an intercompany posting will have empty GSBER

annotations.finding: GGB1 Step 003 sets GSBER=PAR unconditionally for TV docs. But it runs inside the BAPI simulation â€” the BAPI mandatory field check may reject BEFORE this step completes.
annotations.impact: Safety net exists but may fire too late

annotations.finding: GGB0 validation ZFI020: checks GSBER <> DAE/GEF/MBF/OPF/PFF. Empty GSBER PASSES this check. The message text says only use PAR/IBA/FEL but thats not what the code checks.
annotations.impact: Error message misleads â€” suggests wrong BusA value, but the real problem is empty BusA

annotations.finding: 316K vendors in P01. KTOKK field determines LFB1.AKONT (reconciliation GL). Wrong KTOKK = wrong GL = GGB1 miss.
316K vendors but only 559 BUT000 records and 0 CVI_VEND_LINK rows. BP conversion is less than 1% complete.
AKONT (reconciliation GL) is determined by LFA1.KTOKK (vendor account group). AKONT drives which GGB1 substitution rules match.
Subtype 02 (cost distribution) for traveler 10133079 expired 2021-01-31. BAdI ZCL_IM_TRIP_POST_FI reads this subtype â€” expired data = silent failure.
annotations.impact: Any employee with expired PA0027-02 who travels intercompany will have no BusA fallback

annotations.finding: Trip cost assignment table. COMP_CODE field drives same-company vs intercompany posting. Trip 0101004544 had COMP_CODE=UNES (first time for this traveler) â€” caused the failure.
IIEP has zero global rules. All 12 entries restricted to doc type Z1 â€” do NOT apply to TV/TF travel documents.
47 vendor account group definitions. KTOKK=UNES maps to AKONT=2021042 (covered by GGB1). KTOKK=SCSA maps to AKONT=2021011 (NOT covered).
BSEG is declustered in P01. Can read via RFC_READ_TABLE. No MANDT in WHERE clause.
BSEG is NOT a table to extract â€” it is a JOIN via bseg_union VIEW (BSIS+BSAS+BSIK+BSAK+BSID+BSAD). 4.7M rows. Never extract or enrich BSEG directly.
annotations.impact: Extracting BSEG wastes time and produces incomplete data. Use bseg_union VIEW in Gold DB.

annotations.finding: FMIFIIT.KNBELNR = BKPF.BELNR â€” perfect 1:1 FM-to-FI join at line level.
annotations.impact: This is the bridge between Funds Management and Financial Accounting. Critical for Golden Query.

annotations.finding: FMIFIIT.OBJNRZ = PRPS.OBJNR â€” WBS element recovery. 85.9% coverage for 2025.
FM columns differ from FI: FKBTR (not HSL), PERIO (not POPER), FMBELNR (not BELNR).
annotations.impact: Wrong field names = empty results. Always PRAGMA table_info first.

annotations.finding: Account 10xxxxx = permanent bank ledger (by design, never cleared). Account 11xxxxx = clearing sub-account. Real unreconciled = 2,737 items on 11xxxxx (0.6% gap).
annotations.impact: Session #029 found FEBEP=0 was WRONG for 4 sessions. Always verify claims against actual data.

annotations.finding: 223,710 items in FEBEP_2024_2026 (27 fields). The unsuffixed FEBEP (50K/104cols) is truncated first attempt. Always use _2024_2026 suffix.
FPAYP.XREF3 carries the PurposeCode value for DMEE tree SEPA payments. Read by DMEE node N_9662041050. Not a static literal â€” runtime field mapping.
annotations.impact: This is the source of the SEPA Purpose Code that was hunted for 5+ sessions.

annotations.finding: 7.8M rows. PBC (OBJECTCLAS=FMRESERV) generates ~90% of volume. Filter FMRESERV for non-FM analysis.
annotations.impact: Unfiltered CDHDR queries will timeout or return misleading results dominated by FM reservations.

annotations.finding: House bank master: BUKRS+HBKID key. 211 entries across 9 company codes. Links to T012K (bank accounts), T012T (texts).
T012K extracted without UKONT field (sub-bank GL paired with bank GL). Need re-extraction with all fields to validate 10xxx-11xxx pairing.
3,394 same-user BCM batches (creator=approver). Top: C_LOPEZ 94.7%, I_MARQUAND 92.9% self-approval. HQ treasury manual Wednesday cycle, no 3rd operator.
annotations.impact: Dual-control gap â€” automation debt, not fraud. $656M exposure.

annotations.finding: 512-byte line buffer limit. Wide tables need field splitting (MAX_FIELDS_PER_CALL=8). DATA_BUFFER_EXCEEDED = split fields. TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA = empty period (normal).
Pagination bug: when row count exceeds ~50K, some rows silently dropped. Workaround: extract by DAY when pagination fails.
GGB1 substitution exit. Form U910 calls YCL_FI_ACCOUNT_SUBST_READ which reads YTFI_BA_SUBST (range-based, 129 entries) with fallback to YBASUBST (flat, 752 entries).

Custom form pool registered via T80D ARBGB='GBLS' (substitution) AND ARBGB='GBLR' (validation) for the FI module. Both the GGB1 substitution framework (SUBSTID='UNESCO') and the GGB0 validation framework (VALID='UNES') dispatch exit-call steps to forms in this pool. Forms include UXR1/UXR2/UZLS (XREF office tagging), U915/U916/U917 (validation checks), UAEP/UATF (asset), U910 (business area), U901/U902/U904/U908 (treasury/payment), UIT1-4 (ICTP MM postings).
UXR1 guard commented out (line 998) while UXR2 guard active (line 1026) — silent overwrite of user-typed XREF1. Asymmetric defect, root cause of DQ-019 systemic drift.
annotations.impact: Every FI posting by a user with USR05.Y_USERFO populated has XREF1 silently overwritten, regardless of whether the user typed a value. Post-posting manual edits (FB02) become the de-facto correction mechanism.

annotations.finding: DOUBLE asymmetry UXR1 vs UXR2 confirmed at config + code level. UXR1 has NO declarative prereq in GB901 (fires for every BSEG line) + code guard commented at line 998 (overwrites user input). UXR2 has prereq HKONT IN (6 bank GLs) + code guard active at line 1026. Combined: XREF1 drift is systemic (every posting), XREF2 drift is account-scoped.
annotations.impact: Universal overwrite of XREF1 on every UNESCO posting with USR05.Y_USERFO. Users correcting via FBL3N/FB02 = de-facto reconciliation mechanism (21,754 edits Q1 2026 per DQ-019).

annotations.finding: 57 transports modified YRGGBS00 between 2002-2026. Primary maintainer: I_KONAKOV (~20 transports). Substitution changes clustered in KONAKOV 2009-08-27 (D01K951407/442/618) + 2010 (D01K960319). Guard comment-out narrow-scoped to this era.
annotations.impact: Knowing primary maintainer + timing scope enables targeted SE38 version diff for KU-030 definitive closure.

annotations.finding: 13 UNESCO DMEE trees in P01 (8,308 nodes). D01 vs P01: 12/13 identical, only /CGI_XML_CT_UNESCO_1 diverges (9 address nodes).
848K P2P events from EKKO+EKPO+EKBE+ESSR+ESLL+EBAN+RBKP+RSEG. Process mining dashboard built.
4-stream payment model: 1.4M events, 550K cases, 9 company codes. Stream 1=domestic, 2=field office, 3=netting, 4=tier3.
HR org assignment. GSBER field = employee Business Area. GEBER = fund. Fund change from IIEP to UNES fund triggered INC-000006073.

HR master Organizational Assignment infotype. At UNESCO, PA0001.WERKS (personnel area, e.g., FR00=France, ID00=Indonesia, CM00=Cameroon) + PA0001.BTRTL (personnel subarea, e.g., PAR=Paris, JKT=Jakarta, YAO=Yaoundé, KBL=Kabul, DKR=Dakar) is the HR-authoritative office identifier. NOT synchronized with USR05.Y_USERFO — users can drift from HR without detection. Cross-reference via PA0105 SUBTY='0001' USRID=SY-UNAME → PERNR → PA0001.
annotations.impact: The audit query PA0001 × PA0105 × USR05 is the definitive class-generalization tool for detecting drifted office codes (KU-028).

annotations.finding: 239 RFC destinations connecting 38 systems. 7 UNESCO .NET apps (334 RFC-enabled FMs): SISTER, HR Workflow, CMT, UBO Field Office, Travel, Mouv, Procurement.

Line 94-101 hardcodes C_FMDERIVE-FUND_CENTER='UNESCO' for HKONT in (0006045011,0007045011,0006045014) x FIKRS=UNES x FISTL=SPACE. No SY-UNAME or USR05 branch. Author SROCHA 16.11.2010. Spronk removed 6044011 on 18.02.2021.
annotations.impact: Every non-HQ UNES user gets FICTR='UNESCO' on bank-clearing rows of F-53/FBZ2/F110 - 187,514 rows affected across 261 users with mixed-FICTR posting history
annotations.session_note: [Session #51 triage] Originally captured by INC-000005240 Session #50 subagent pursuing FMDERIVE/ZXFMDTU02_RPY FICTR='UNESCO' hypothesis that was LATER REJECTED as the wrong mechanism for INC-000005240 (the real root cause is XREF1/XREF2 substitution via YRGGBS00 UXR1/UXR2 — see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md). The finding itself may be a real standalone observation — filed as knowledge/observations/obs_fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_gl_6045xxx.md. Do NOT link this entry to INC-000005240.

annotations.finding: File contains 10+ hardcoded C_FMDERIVE-FUND_CENTER and C_FMDERIVE-FUND assignments (lines 85,99,125,137,158,162,179,183,204-216). Each is a potential INC-000005240 class incident waiting to surface.
annotations.impact: Hidden systemic debt: any new reporting practice that groups by FICTR/FOND will expose one of these.
annotations.session_note: [Session #51 triage] Originally captured by INC-000005240 Session #50 subagent pursuing FMDERIVE/ZXFMDTU02_RPY FICTR='UNESCO' hypothesis that was LATER REJECTED as the wrong mechanism for INC-000005240 (the real root cause is XREF1/XREF2 substitution via YRGGBS00 UXR1/UXR2 — see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md). The finding itself may be a real standalone observation — filed as knowledge/observations/obs_fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_gl_6045xxx.md. Do NOT link this entry to INC-000005240.

annotations.finding: Authored by S. ROCHA (2010) and I. KONAKOV (2015-2025). Style: nested CASE on FIKRS/GSBER/HKONT with literal string targets. Never refactored into FMDERIVE customer rules despite SROCHA26072010 comment 'Replaced by Derivation Rule' suggesting the intent was to migrate out.
annotations.session_note: [Session #51 triage] Originally captured by INC-000005240 Session #50 subagent pursuing FMDERIVE/ZXFMDTU02_RPY FICTR='UNESCO' hypothesis that was LATER REJECTED as the wrong mechanism for INC-000005240 (the real root cause is XREF1/XREF2 substitution via YRGGBS00 UXR1/UXR2 — see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md). The finding itself may be a real standalone observation — filed as knowledge/observations/obs_fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_gl_6045xxx.md. Do NOT link this entry to INC-000005240.

annotations.finding: 187,514 rows match signature BUKRS=UNES AND FISTL='UNESCO' AND HKONT in (0006045011,0007045011,0006045014) - the hardcode class from INC-000005240
annotations.impact: Zero rows have FISTL='JAK' on these GLs - the user's expectation is code-impossible under the current exit
annotations.session_note: [Session #51 triage] Originally captured by INC-000005240 Session #50 subagent pursuing FMDERIVE/ZXFMDTU02_RPY FICTR='UNESCO' hypothesis that was LATER REJECTED as the wrong mechanism for INC-000005240 (the real root cause is XREF1/XREF2 substitution via YRGGBS00 UXR1/UXR2 — see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md). The finding itself may be a real standalone observation — filed as knowledge/observations/obs_fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_gl_6045xxx.md. Do NOT link this entry to INC-000005240.

annotations.finding: 764 rows / 710 distinct FICTR in 7 FM areas. UNES has JAK (Jakarta), HQC (HQ Central), HQD (HQ Delegation) plus 700+ more field offices. The hardcode C_FMDERIVE-FUND_CENTER='UNESCO' in ZXFMDTU02_RPY:99 overrides all 700+ possibilities with one literal.
annotations.session_note: [Session #51 triage] Originally captured by INC-000005240 Session #50 subagent pursuing FMDERIVE/ZXFMDTU02_RPY FICTR='UNESCO' hypothesis that was LATER REJECTED as the wrong mechanism for INC-000005240 (the real root cause is XREF1/XREF2 substitution via YRGGBS00 UXR1/UXR2 — see knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md). The finding itself may be a real standalone observation — filed as knowledge/observations/obs_fmderive_hardcoded_fictr_gl_6045xxx.md. Do NOT link this entry to INC-000005240.

annotations.finding: USR05 (SAP user parameters, PARID/PARVA per user) not in Gold DB. Cannot verify whether AL_JONATHAN has a home FICTR parameter. Needed to design the INC-000005240 fix (SU3-based opt-in).

User parameters table (SU3). At UNESCO, the Y_USERFO parameter on USR05 drives office-code substitution on BSEG.XREF1/XREF2 via YRGGBS00 forms UXR1/UXR2. There is NO mechanism that keeps USR05.Y_USERFO in sync with HR master (PA0001.WERKS/BTRTL). A user's finance office code can drift from their HR personnel area with no detection.

Registered at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:230 as substitution exit 'UXR1' (XREF1 field substitution). Body at lines 996-1016. Unconditionally reads USR05 WHERE BNAME=SY-UNAME AND PARID='Y_USERFO' and writes the value to bseg_xref1. Original guard 'IF bseg_xref1 = space' at line 998 is commented out — UXR1 ALWAYS overwrites, even if the user typed a value on the screen. After writing, validates against YFO_CODES.FOCOD and raises w018 ZFI warning (non-blocking) if missing.
annotations.impact: Writes posting user's office code to every BSEG line at callpoint 3 regardless of TCODE/KOART. Has an in-code dictionary check but it only catches dictionary misses, not wrong-but-valid codes.

annotations.finding: Registered at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:235 as substitution exit 'UXR2' (XREF2 field substitution). Body at lines 1024-1041. ASYMMETRIC VALIDATION: (a) IF bseg_xref2 = space (auto-write branch from USR05) — NO validation against YFO_CODES, trusts USR05 blindly. (b) ELSE branch (user typed a value on screen) — SELECT YFO_CODES and raise e018 ZFI HARD error if missing. This asymmetry means wrong master data (USR05) propagates silently while manual typing errors are blocked. On F-53, the screen hides XREF2 so UXR2 always takes the SPACE branch → no validation.
annotations.impact: Is the main vector for AL_JONATHAN-class master-data errors propagating to XREF2 silently. The asymmetric validation design was likely intended to protect against typos, but it fails to protect against stale user parameters.

annotations.finding: Registered at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:240 as substitution exit 'UZLS' (ZLSCH field substitution). Body at lines 1050-1119. Derives BSEG-ZLSCH (payment method) from BSEG-XREF2 per company code. Special case: IF bseg-xref2(4) <> 'UNDP' → CASE bukrs... ENDCASE. For UNES, XREF2='HQ' keeps ZLSCH at default; non-HQ forces ZLSCH='O' (field-office outbound). Similar rules for UBO/BRZ, UIS/UIS, IBE/IBE, IIEP/IIEP_PAR. UNDP-prefixed XREF2 gets ZLSCH='U'. This is the downstream consumer of UXR2's output — wrong XREF2 causes wrong payment method routing at F110/BCM time.
annotations.impact: Wrong XREF2 → wrong ZLSCH → wrong payment method → wrong DMEE format → wrong house bank → misrouted payment. Single-point chain.

annotations.finding: Validation exit (VALID='UNES' VALSEQNR=011, CHECKID='2UNES###011'). Body at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1499-1519. Returns b_false if the vendor has >1 bank account in LFBK and no BVTYP is specified on the line. Skips BSCHL 26/39 (down-payment clearing). Fires only on invoice-entry TCODEs via CONDID '1UNES###011' (invoice TCODE list + KOART='K'). Does NOT reference or check BSEG-XREF fields.
annotations.impact: Protects against ambiguous vendor bank selection but does NOT protect against wrong XREF values. Not a useful safety net for XREF-related incidents.

annotations.finding: Validation exit (VALID='UNES' VALSEQNR=009, CHECKID='2UNES###012'). Body at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1525-1541. Fund GL restriction: for FISTL='UNESCO' allows HKONT in a fixed list of 6 GLs, otherwise requires HKONT='0006043011'. Severity I (informational only — does not block). Does NOT reference or check BSEG-XREF fields.

Validation exit (VALID='UNES' VALSEQNR=012, CHECKID='2UNES###009'). Body at YRGGBS00_SOURCE.txt:1543-1590. Checks SCB (State Central Bank) indicator LZBKZ consistency with vendor's bank country. Reads LFBK.BANKS; if LZBKZ is filled, its first 2 chars must match. If LZBKZ empty, checks YTFI_PPC_STRUC for PPC_VAR/PPC_DESCR requirement. Skips BSCHL 26/39. Fires only on invoice-entry TCODEs via CONDID '1UNES###009'. Does NOT reference or check BSEG-XREF fields.
annotations.impact: Protects against SCB indicator mismatches for cross-border vendor payments but does NOT protect against wrong XREF values.

annotations.finding: SU3 parameter on USR05 containing the UNESCO office code for the user (HQ, JAK, YAO, KAB, DAK, BRZ, IIEP_PAR, UIS, IBE, etc.). Read by YRGGBS00 forms UXR1, UXR2 (and the commented-out legacy UZLS branch) at FI posting time to populate BSEG-XREF1/BSEG-XREF2 with the originating office tag. Maintained via SU01/SU3 as free-text — no validation at maintenance time, no HR alignment, no automated drift detection. This is the single driver of AL_JONATHAN's INC-000005240 ticket.
annotations.impact: HIGH — any drift between this parameter and the user's actual office silently mis-tags every FI document they post. No runtime validation anywhere in the UNES framework catches wrong values.

annotations.finding: UNESCO custom whitelist table for valid Field Office codes. Minimum columns FOCOD (office code) + PAYMT (payment method). Used by YRGGBS00 UXR1 (after writing XREF1, validates against FOCOD; warns w018 ZFI if missing) and YRGGBS00 UXR2 (only in the user-typed ELSE branch; hard error e018 ZFI if missing). Not in Gold DB (KU-027). Contents not yet verified — the JAK code's presence is critical to the AL_JONATHAN fix. Maintained by UNESCO BASIS via SM30.
annotations.impact: If FOCOD='JAK' is missing when AL_JONATHAN's Y_USERFO is updated to 'JAK', UXR1 will raise w018 warnings on every posting and UXR2 ELSE branch will throw e018 hard errors if he ever manually types 'JAK' on an invoice screen.

annotations.finding: Personnel subarea code. At UNESCO, this is the HR-authoritative office code. Observed values and mapping to USR05.Y_USERFO: PAR→HQ (Paris), JKT→JAK (Jakarta), YAO→YAO (Yaoundé, match), KBL→KAB (Kabul, mismatch), DKR→DAK (Dakar, mismatch). The BTRTL→Y_USERFO mapping is tribal knowledge — NOT enforced by any table. This is DQ-020.

HR Communications infotype. SUBTY='0001' rows contain USRID = the user's SAP BNAME. This is the canonical bridge from SY-UNAME → PERNR for any HR cross-reference. Used in the INC-000005240 PA0001 lookups to get AL_JONATHAN's personnel area/subarea from his BNAME.

GGB0 validation rule at BOOLCLASS=009 (FI line item). Created 2007-09-05 by M_SPRONK, last changed 2026-10-11 by FP_SPEZZANO. Has 12 steps in GB931 (extracted via RFC Session #51). Full CONDID/CHECKID/severity/message map in knowledge/incidents/INC-000005240_xref_office_substitution.md §3.4.3. **NONE of the 12 steps reference BSEG-XREF1 or BSEG-XREF2.** The validation checks GSBER, BLART, TCODE, HKONT, GEBER, BSCHL, bank account count (U915), SCB indicator (U917), and fund GL (U916) — but zero XREF content. F-53/FBZ2 is NOT in any prerequisite — manual outgoing payments are completely outside the UNES validation layer.
annotations.impact: The validation rule exists but has a critical scope gap: no XREF check and no F-53 coverage. This is why wrong XREF values commit silently on manual payments.

annotations.finding: GGB1 substitution rule at callpoint 3 (complete document). 17 steps in GB922 (Gold DB confirmed). Steps include: 001 (HKONT via UGLS), 002 (GSBER via U910), 003 (BVTYP/PYCUR via U901/U902), 004 (GSBER='GEF'), 005 (XREF1 via UXR1), 006 (XREF2 via UXR2), 007 (ZLSCH via UZLS), 008 (UAEP asset), 009 (HKONT='2021023'), 010 (GSBER via U904), 011-012 (FDLEV/ZLSPR), 013 (FDLEV via U908), 014-015 (ZUONR/ZLSPR), 016 (UATF). GB905/GB921 are empty at UNESCO — step-to-prerequisite linkage is NOT in standard tables. Step prerequisites are likely implicit via naming convention 3UNESCO#X → GB901, with empty entries meaning 'no gate / fires unconditionally'.

GGB0 validation rule header. 17 rows at UNESCO P01 (RFC confirmed Session #51). Key row: VALID='UNES' BOOLCLASS='009' MSGID='ZFI'. NOT in Gold DB — should be extracted for future FI validation incidents.

GGB0 validation steps. 53 rows total; 12 rows for VALID='UNES' (RFC confirmed Session #51). Fields: VALID, VALSEQNR, CONDID, CHECKID, VALSEVERE, VALMSG, MSGTAB1-4, MSGFIELD1-4. The full 12-step UNES validation map is in INC-000005240 §3.4.3. NOT in Gold DB — should be extracted.

Substitution step header — expected to link GB922 SUBSTID+SUBSEQNR to a BOOLID prerequisite. Returns ZERO rows via RFC_READ_TABLE at UNESCO P01 broad probe (Session #51). Either empty (UNESCO doesn't use standard GGB1 step-header mechanism) or inaccessible via RFC. This is KU-031. Practical consequence: substitution step prerequisites must be discovered via empirical CDPOS testing or GGB1 GUI inspection.
Substitution rule header — expected to store SUBSTID + callpoint + BOOLCLASS. Returns ZERO rows via RFC_READ_TABLE at UNESCO P01 (Session #51). Same status as GB905 — either empty or inaccessible.

12-char Reference Key 1 field on BSEG. SAP leaves it for customer use. At UNESCO, repurposed as the originating office tag on vendor lines via YRGGBS00 form UXR1 (reads USR05.Y_USERFO and writes unconditionally — original guard commented out). Observed values: HQ (Paris), JAK (Jakarta), YAO (Yaoundé), KAB (Kabul), DAK (Dakar), BRZ (Brasilia), IIEP_PAR, UIS, IBE. NOT carried in Gold DB bseg_union view — must read via RFC on BSAK/BSAS/BSIS/BSIK/BSAD/BSID.

12-char Reference Key 2 field on BSEG. At UNESCO, overloaded: (a) on most UNES vendor lines it carries the same office code as XREF1 via UXR2 auto-write; (b) on UNDP transfer transactions it carries 'UNDP-*' memo references (UXR2 recognizes this via UZLS special case IF bseg-xref2(4)<>'UNDP'); (c) on MIRO/FB60 invoices where the screen exposes XREF2 the user can enter custom values that survive UXR2's ELSE branch (subject to e018 YFO_CODES hard error check). NOT in Gold DB bseg_union.

Payment method field on BSEG. At UNESCO, derived from BSEG-XREF2 per company code via YRGGBS00 form UZLS: for UNES XREF2='HQ' keeps default, non-HQ forces 'O' (field-office outbound); for UBO 'BRZ', UIS 'UIS', IBE 'IBE', IIEP 'IIEP_PAR'. UNDP special case sets 'U'. Wrong XREF2 → wrong ZLSCH → wrong downstream payment routing (F110, BCM, DMEE, house bank).

Constructor (CM00A line 8) hardcodes mr_waers = ['EUR']. This is the gate that blocks cross-currency consumption from getting BR treatment in Camp B enhancements. mr_waers2 (Staff variant) includes both EUR + USD but is dead code (only called from IF 1=2 branches). The complete cross-currency method convert_to_currency_2 (CM002) exists, is production-transported, but never runs.
annotations.impact: Every USD consumption posting against an EUR-loaded BR fund silently bypasses BR conversion and persists at identity, drifting the AVC pool fund-by-fund until FMAVC005 fires.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_CHECK_CONS is Camp A: gate input iv_waers reads the FR's own currency (kblk-waers/kbld-waers). Always passes for EUR FRs regardless of consumption posting currency. SAFE for the cross-currency bug — it does not contribute to drift. CHECK_CONS specifically uses SAVE/RESTORE pattern (Enh1 saves m_t_addref, recalcs at BR; Enh2 restores) — nothing persisted, only EF check sees BR.
annotations.impact: Reading these enhancements alone gives a misleading picture that BR works correctly. The bug is in Camp B (NEW_ITEM/AVC/KBLEW/FI/PAYCOMMIT) which use posting-currency for the gate.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_EF_FUND_RESERVATION is Camp A: gate input iv_waers reads the FR's own currency (kblk-waers/kbld-waers). Always passes for EUR FRs regardless of consumption posting currency. SAFE for the cross-currency bug — it does not contribute to drift. CHECK_CONS specifically uses SAVE/RESTORE pattern (Enh1 saves m_t_addref, recalcs at BR; Enh2 restores) — nothing persisted, only EF check sees BR.
annotations.impact: Reading these enhancements alone gives a misleading picture that BR works correctly. The bug is in Camp B (NEW_ITEM/AVC/KBLEW/FI/PAYCOMMIT) which use posting-currency for the gate.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_FUNDBLOCK is Camp A: gate input iv_waers reads the FR's own currency (kblk-waers/kbld-waers). Always passes for EUR FRs regardless of consumption posting currency. SAFE for the cross-currency bug — it does not contribute to drift. CHECK_CONS specifically uses SAVE/RESTORE pattern (Enh1 saves m_t_addref, recalcs at BR; Enh2 restores) — nothing persisted, only EF check sees BR.
annotations.impact: Reading these enhancements alone gives a misleading picture that BR works correctly. The bug is in Camp B (NEW_ITEM/AVC/KBLEW/FI/PAYCOMMIT) which use posting-currency for the gate.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_AVC is Camp B: gate input iv_waers reads the per-row posting currency (twaer/<*>-waers). FAILS silently when consumption is non-EUR. Standard SAP then persists the row at identity (FKBTR=TRBTR for USD) or at standard M-rate. This is the silent drift-injector.
annotations.impact: Each non-EUR consumption posting that hits this enhancement contributes drift to the AVC pool. Across 458 funds in P01, lifetime drift volume is ~$375M with ~$35.7M estimated drift exposure.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_FI is Camp B: gate input iv_waers reads the per-row posting currency (twaer/<*>-waers). FAILS silently when consumption is non-EUR. Standard SAP then persists the row at identity (FKBTR=TRBTR for USD) or at standard M-rate. This is the silent drift-injector.
annotations.impact: Each non-EUR consumption posting that hits this enhancement contributes drift to the AVC pool. Across 458 funds in P01, lifetime drift volume is ~$375M with ~$35.7M estimated drift exposure.

annotations.finding: Camp C: filter-only enhancement, no BR conversion. Fires only on FMN4N (PO commitment recalc). DELETES rows from t_fmoi/u_t_fmioi_buf where fikrs<>'UNES' OR twaer<>'EUR' OR bus_area<>'GEF' OR ftype not in mr_fund_type. Effect: revaluation excludes USD rows on EUR-loaded funds → drift cleanup tools cannot fix the cross-currency gap.
annotations.impact: If you try to run FMN4N to clean up drift, this enhancement removes the very rows that need cleaning. Need to either temporarily deactivate this enhancement or extend H39 to handle USD rows on EUR-loaded funds.

annotations.finding: Camp C: filter-only. Fires on FMZZ (Reservation Fund Revaluation report). Sets testlauf='X' (TEST RUN ONLY — never persists). Filters non-EUR rows out. Inert in production except for reporting purposes.

BAdI implementation of FMAVC_ENTRY_FILTER. Created 2025-05-14 in D01 (transports D01K9B0D4Z + D01K9B0D50). Deactivated next day 2025-05-15 (transports D01K9B0D54 + D01K9B0D55). Exists only in D01 + V01 — absent from TS1, TS3, P01. Logic only in BUDGET_FILTER (wrong method — should be POSTING_FILTER for consumption); whitelist hardcoded to MIRO + F110. POSTING_FILTER is empty stub. NEVER affected production AVC behavior. PMO H38 to delete from D01+V01.
annotations.impact: Do NOT cite this BAdI as evidence that 'MIRO/F110 are silenced in production'. They are not. AVC fires equally for all tcodes in P01.

annotations.finding: ZFIX_FM_BR_POST_FROM_PY_AUTO is Camp D: payroll/BAPI bridge. Out of scope for the cross-currency bug. Handles HRPAY runid bookkeeping and BAPI account data setting respectively.
ZFIX_BR_BAPI_EMPLOYEE_POST is Camp D: payroll/BAPI bridge. Out of scope for the cross-currency bug. Handles HRPAY runid bookkeeping and BAPI account data setting respectively.

Wide table (>512 bytes/row). RFC_READ_TABLE FIELDS=[] returns DATA_BUFFER_EXCEEDED. Workaround: pass narrow FIELDS list (max 12 cols) AND split WHERE conditions into multiple OPTIONS entries (single complex WHERE rejected with OPTION_NOT_VALID). Dedup result rows by full-row signature (RVERS/RPMAX duplicates double-count if summed naively). For UNESCO ledger 9H 2026 carryforward allotment: ALLOCTYPE_9='KBFC'. HSL01-HSL16 = per-period local USD; HSLVT = yearly total. Available = SUM(HSL*) - SUM(open FMIOI commitments + FMIFIIT actuals on same FONDS+FUNDSCTR+CMMTITEM).
annotations.impact: If you don't dedup or you sum HSLVT alongside HSL01-16, you double-count budget. Vonthron's case: $12,897.80 was the correct dedup'd value; without dedup it became $25,795.60 (2x). That misled my analysis briefly.

annotations.finding: Earmarked-fund commitment rows: original creation (positive), period-specific consumption reductions (negative), period-016 year-end reversal (positive equal to remaining), period-000 carryforward-IN to next year. Naive sign-sum across all years does NOT equal 'open balance'. For 'open' filter by current GJAHR + signed sum. For 'lifetime consumed' use KBLE/KBLEW NOT FMIOI. Rows with ratio=1.0 + amount $0.01-0.02 are SAP rounding plugs, NOT cross-currency consumption signatures.
annotations.impact: 3 wrong interpretations in Session #053 came from skipping raw-row inspection on FMIOI. Always sample 3 raw WRTTP=81 rows for any FR before computing per-line metrics.

annotations.finding: Cluster table — RFC_READ_TABLE returns TABLE_WITHOUT_DATA. Use RFC-enabled wrapper FM '/SAPPSPRO/PD_GM_FMR2_READ_KBLE' with parameter I_BELNR=<FR doc number>. Returns T_KBLE (consumption events with RBELNR/RBUZEI = consumer FI doc/line — the canonical FR↔FI traceability link without needing BSEG.KBLNR access) AND T_KBLEW (per-currency split CURTP=00 transaction currency, CURTP=10 local USD).
annotations.impact: Without this wrapper FM, FR consumption history is unreachable via standard RFC. Use it whenever you need to know which FI documents consumed a specific FR line.

annotations.finding: Cluster table. Same access pattern as KBLE — use /SAPPSPRO/PD_GM_FMR2_READ_KBLE wrapper which returns T_KBLEW alongside T_KBLE. Each KBLE consumption event produces 2 KBLEW rows: POSNR=01 CURTP=00 (transaction currency, EUR for EUR FR), POSNR=02 CURTP=10 (local currency = USD = FM-area). The CURTP=10 USD value is the BR-applied USD-equivalent of the consumption (if BR fired correctly).
annotations.impact: To find the per-line BR-applied consumption USD value, sum WRBTR where CURTP=10 grouped by REFBN+RFPOS.

annotations.finding: Standard SAP class. Method CHECK_CONSUMPTION is the natural hook for the preventive validation (PMO H39): block any consumption posting where FR header WAERS=EUR + GSBER=GEF + fund_type ∈ mr_fund_type AND consumption_doc-WAERS ≠ EUR with MESSAGE 'Z_BR_001' TYPE 'E'. ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE_CHECK_CONS already enhances this method — H39 adds a sibling enhancement that runs BEFORE the recalc and rejects cross-currency upfront.
